text:
The goal bearing is +177°, which is nearly directly behind the current yaw of 0°. This means the target is almost 180° away — effectively "behind" me.

However, the distance is very large (7298 cm), so I should not stop yet.

Given that the goal is behind me, turning around is necessary. Since +177° is slightly to the right of directly behind (which would be 180°), a TURN_RIGHT might help align me better toward the goal after rotation.

But note: if I turn right 30°, my new yaw becomes +30°, and the relative bearing to goal becomes 177 - 30 = +147° — still behind but less so. Alternatively, turning left would make it worse (177 + 30 = 207° → equivalent to -153°, even more behind).

Actually, since 177° is only 3° off from 180°, the most efficient move is to turn around — but we can only turn 30° at a time. So either TURN_LEFT or TURN_RIGHT will get us closer to facing the goal.
